dependency_notes: S2 depends on three real (non-mocked) exports S1 lands first: confirmContact(pubkeyHex), the shared pending-confirmation predicate, and ContactListItem.isPendingConfirmation, all from app/src/lib/contacts.ts. Because story implementation is strictly sequential (S1 before S2, per project convention — see owning_module principle), S2 consumes S1's real implementation directly; no temporary mock is introduced for this seam. S1 has no dependency on S2 and is independently testable via its own unit suites (contacts.test.ts, contacts-selectable.test.ts, contacts-add-by-npub.test.ts, pairingAck.test.ts) without any S2 code existing.
mock_resolution_plan: None required. The only cross-story seam (S1 -> S2) is resolved by implementation order, not by mocking.

seams.data: Pending-confirmation primitives exported by contacts.ts: the confirm action, the shared pure predicate, and the derived list-item flag.

source.artifact: app/src/lib/contacts.ts: confirmContact(pubkeyHex), the exported pending-confirmation predicate (single export site per AC-STRUCT-3), ContactListItem.isPendingConfirmation

destination.artifact: app/src/lib/directMessageNotifications.ts bell-bump gate (kind4Handler/kind1059Handler); the contacts.tsx / ContactDetailView three-way mount decision and contacts-list badge; the confirm-prompt component's onConfirm handler

invariants: isPendingConfirmation reads only from readStoredContacts() or an equivalent explicit parameter, never from any other store (AC-STRUCT-3).
isPendingConfirmation MUST NOT call, or be folded into, isAllowedDmSenderComposite / isAllowedDmSender / isBlockedPeer (AC-STRUCT-3, ADR-008 documented exception).
isPendingConfirmation and confirmContact resolve pubkeyHex against stored keys case-insensitively, mirroring addContactByNpub's matchingKeys pattern (AC-STRUCT-4).
confirmContact(pubkeyHex) is a no-op — no throw, no storage write — for an unknown pubkey or a pubkey whose pendingConfirmationSince is already null (AC-CONFIRM-2).
confirmContact(pubkeyHex) clears pendingConfirmationSince to null and MUST NOT modify firstSeenAt, lastSeenAt, or archivedAt on that entry (AC-CONFIRM-1).

stories.name: pending-admission-core
stories.title: Pending Admission Core
stories.description: Adds the StoredContact.pendingConfirmationSince field and its default-null normalization, the pending-admission primitive used only by handlePairingAck's Step 9 for a brand-new sender (re-pairing preserves the existing value, mirroring archivedAt), confirmContact(pubkeyHex), the single exported pending-confirmation predicate that S2's call sites will import, and the selectableContactsForGroup disabledReason precedence extension (already_member > blocked > pending_confirmation > selectable). Also covers the wire-level non-regression guarantee that admission and confirmation never publish kind-0 or otherwise change the existing gift-wrap/name-drop side effects.
stories.notes: Touches pairingAck.ts as a one-line call-site swap (rememberContact -> the new pending-admission primitive at Step 9 only; Steps 10-11 and rememberKnownPeers are unchanged, Design Decisions 4 and 7) rather than as a second owned module — pairingAck.ts owns no data of its own for this epic (architecture.md module map: 'none, orchestration only') and is purely a consumer of the primitive this story defines, so it stays inside S1 rather than forming a third story. AC-STRUCT-3 is defined and given its one canonical export here; S2 verifies the 'no inline re-derivation' clause again at its own call sites (bell gate, render/mount gate) but does not redefine or re-export the predicate. Case-insensitive matching (AC-STRUCT-4) must reuse contacts.ts's existing matchingKeys pattern (contacts.ts:399), per architecture.md Implementation constraints and prior learning storedcontact-keys-lowercase-only-via-transitive in exploration.json — do not assume stored keys are lowercase.

stories.name: message-hold-bell-and-confirmation-ui
stories.title: Message Hold, Bell Delay, and Confirmation UI
stories.description: Gates the notification bell (directMessageNotifications.ts's kind4Handler/kind1059Handler) on S1's pending predicate before incrementDirectMessage. Holds back a pending contact's rendered thread by not mounting ContactChat while pending, extending contacts.tsx / ContactDetailView's existing blocked/normal branch into a three-way blocked/pending/normal branch (blocked always wins, Design Decision 9), backed by a new pendingConfirmationRevision-equivalent reactive counter so confirming a contact updates the view within the same mounted session with no navigation away and back. Adds the contacts-list pending badge and confirm action, wires it to S1's confirmContact, and triggers bell reconciliation on confirm by reusing unreadStore.ts's existing initDirectMessageCounts with a one-element peer array (not a hand-rolled recount). Adds en/de i18n Copy entries for every new string.
stories.notes: The AC-MSG-2 render hold is implemented as contacts.tsx's ContactDetailView mount decision (ContactChat is simply not mounted while pending, matching the existing isArchived -> BlockedBanner precedent), not as an internal filter inside ContactChat.tsx itself — per exploration.json's architecture finding that no 'visible messages' derivation point exists inside ContactChat today. ContactChat.tsx stays in scope only for the AC-STRUCT-3 import-site check, not for a rendering-logic change; the dev/architect implementing this story should confirm no inline predicate re-derivation is needed there before touching the file. Where exactly the new pendingConfirmationRevision-equivalent counter lives (local useState in contacts.tsx vs. a MarmotContext-hosted counter like blockedPeersRevision) is left to the implementing architect per architecture.md's Seams section — no existing precedent to follow mechanically. Bell reconciliation (AC-OBS-2) must call the existing initDirectMessageCounts([peer], ownPubkeyHex) via directConversationId + chatPersistence.ts#loadMessages (the canonical read path) rather than the raw few:messages:dm:<peer> key initDirectMessageCounts currently builds inline internally — unreadStore.ts itself may need no functional change, only a new call site, per architecture.md's seam note.
